title: ITR filing 2026: Here's what happens if you miss the July 31 deadline — 1% monthly interest, late fee and more

title: ITR filing 2026: Is it necessary to keep physical copies of digital tax documents? Expert explains
summary: ITR filing is now becoming increasingly paperless, but taxpayers must securely retain key digital tax documents. A tax expert explains which records to preserve, why backups matter, and how organised digital files can help during tax verification, assessments and future income tax notices.

title: DA hike: What are the types of Dearness Allowances, how they are calculated — explained
summary: Dearness Allowance is a percentage of employees' pay aimed at helping households manage the increased cost of living. For calculation purposes, it is separated into two categories: industrial and variable DA.

title: Only 30% of individual investors' mutual fund AUM is in direct plans: Why retail buyers still prefer regular route
summary: While direct plans make up 77.7% of institutional investors' mutual fund AUM, the corresponding share for individual investors is just 30%. Experts say the gap is driven by the need for professional advice and investor behaviour.&nbsp;

title: Changed jobs? Your PF won't be transferred immediately — here's what triggers the automatic transfer
summary: Your provident fund won't move instantly after you switch jobs. EPFO's new mechanism auto-triggers transfer after your new employer's first contribution. Details here.

title: BNPL impact on credit score: 5 key facts every borrower should know before using buy now, pay later
summary: A buy now, pay later facility can help build a strong credit history when used responsibly. Timely repayments, controlled borrowing, and avoiding defaults are crucial. Learn how BNPL services affect credit scores, borrowing ability, and financial health with key tips for users.

title: Presumptive taxation: Who should opt for it & who should avoid it? Experts explain
summary: The presumptive taxation scheme offers a simpler way to compute taxable income, but it may not suit every taxpayer. Here's what businesses and professionals should consider before choosing it.

title: How much EPF can you withdraw? Check it online with these simple steps.
summary: The new rules compress a dozen complicated advance clauses into three broad categories while requiring a mandatory 25% retirement reserve.

title: Bought a car worth over ₹10 lakh? Here's how to claim your 1% TCS refund
summary: If you have bought a car worth more than ₹10 lakh, you would have paid a TCS of 1% to the dealer. This is not an additional tax but a tax credit that can be adjusted against your total income tax liability or claimed as a refund while filing the ITR.&nbsp;

title: Filing ITR-1 this year? These new disclosure requirements could affect your return
summary: From reporting income from two house properties to furnishing additional details for donations and rental income, the latest ITR-1 utility brings several changes. Here's what taxpayers need to know before filing.

title: FIIs cut stakes, but these 10 stocks rallied up to 220% in just over 3 months
